title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Sehgal argues that as LLMs commoditize, agent infrastructure differentiation will shift from orchestration layers and tools to durable session logs as the core primitive. He contends that logs enable agent persistence across models and runtimes, drawing an analogy to append-only log databases and video game save files.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara makes the case that durable session logs, not orchestration layers or tools, will become the core primitive that differentiates agent infrastructure as LLMs commoditize. The argument: logs let an agent persist across different models and runtimes, with append-only log databases and video game save files offered as analogies for how that state carries forward. The takeaway is a reframing of where value accrues in the agent stack, away from the orchestration and tooling layers and toward the session log itself.
